summary: [3.x] Visual Server memory leak on Android device

description.text:
### Tested versions

- reproduced: v3.6.stable.official [de2f0f147]
- not: v3.5.3.stable.official [6c814135b]
- not: v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Xiaomi Redmi 5 Plus, Android 8.1, Adreno 506

### Issue description

Found `VisualServer` memory leak introduced between **3.6** and **3.5.2** versions

### Steps to reproduce

0. start logcat with
```
adb logcat -s godot
```

2. make project, add button with following script

summary: Editor wants to reload GDScript files constantly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.beta2.mono.official [a013481b0]
- NOT Reproducible in v4.4.beta1.mono.official [d33da79d3]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta2.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3570K CPU @ 3.40GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

It seems that even if Godot is able to handle external changes to files through the language server, changes are still treated like they were "external changes". Even after told to reload from disk, the file is considered being changed and "Files have been modified outside Godot" dialog is displayed whenever Godot gets focus.

My "Auto Reload Scripts on External Change" setting is disabled (default value). If I change that setting to enabled, the problem goes away, but my understanding is that the language server should handle this situation with the default setting (no auto reload) and auto reload should only be used when the external editor doesn't support language server.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Godot and start a new project
1. Open external editor, eg. vscode. Make sure external editor connects to the language server
1. Keep both Godot and external editor visible
1. Add a GDScript file to the project 
1. Edit GDScript file using the external editor => You can see the change appearing to Godot's code editor as expected
1. Switch focus back to Godot 
=> **"Files have been modified outside Godot" BUG This shouldn't happen.**

1. Click "Reload from disk" 
1. Switch focus out from Godot (doesn't need to focus to the external editor)
1. Switch focus back to Godot
=> **"Files have been modified outside Godot" BUG This happens again.**

summary: Fix animation name replacement logic for scene resource reimporting

description.text:
Sometimes upon invoking "Reimport" on certain external scene resources whose original formats support animations ( and likely specifically after the scene has already been instantiated as a child under another scene's root node ), the following error log message may appear:

```
Invalid animation name: 'my_animation_library_name/my_animation_name'.
```

Such an occurrence is due to how the `AnimationMixer::get_animation_list` method is implemented to return registered animation names with a prefix of the name of the owning animation library if it is not the default library which is nameless ( https://github.com/PierceLBrooks/godot/blob/plb/import-scene-res-anim-name-replacement-bug/scene/animation/animation_mixer.cpp#L146 ).

My changes will resolve this issue.

summary: Fix a crash on iOS from Joypad initialization

description.text:
The way iOS initializes joypads right now will crash the app, if a gamepad is already connected before the game is started.

`JoypadApple` starts listening for joypad notifications before the `Input` singleton is available. iOS will immediately send a notification about controllers already on and connected. This will lead to attempted use of the Input singleton before it's ready ([here is where the crash will happen](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/9ee1873ae1e09c217ac24a5800007f63cb895615/drivers/apple/joypad_apple.mm#L303)). 

`initialize_joypad()` on the other hand is called explicitly after the Input singleton becomes available ([here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/9ee1873ae1e09c217ac24a5800007f63cb895615/main/main.cpp#L2941)).

For context - the issue was introduced [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/cd17cb011075d110d1ea50f8a9c0573b22c3dc58#diff-f0ce1cbd4e741f31a64b71de9cc7633aacc3e1e75b16562b41df7d6142c3af45), and from the context it seems like it was probably an oversight.

**Steps to reproduce**

- Connect a hardware controller to an iOS device
- Launch any Godot app

summary: [Web] `Input.mouse_mode` can't capture mouse after user presses escape key in browser

description.text:

### Issue description

I have discovered an edge case that likely happens often. When the mouse is captured browsers tend to tell the user that they can press escape to free there mouse. If we capture the users mouse and then they press escape we can not again capture it with the current behavior. Even though the engine knows that the mouse has been released. To capture the mouse again after the user has pressed escape we must first tell the engine to release the mouse and then capture it again. So that they engine executes the correct sequence.

Note: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/100758#issuecomment-2624679816

### Steps to reproduce


1. Extract MRP
2. Open MRP in Godot 4.4.beta2
3. Ensure exports are managed for Godot 4.4.beta2
4. Run the MRP in the browser.
5. Capture the mouse using the button provided.
6. Press the escape key "esc".
7. Observe that your mouse is released and visible.
8. Attempt to capture the mouse using the button provided that says "Capture Mouse".
9. Observe this does not work.
10. Press the button that says "Capture Mouse After Release".
11. Observe this works.

Alternatively for step 10. You can also press space bar or press the "Release Mouse" button. and then you can press the "Capture Mouse" button and it will work.

summary: Wasteful idiom in the Godot Editor: monitoring every node removed across various plugins.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.3, Godot/master

### System information

MacOS 

### Issue description

There is an idiom in the Editor where plugins connect to the SceneTree "node_removed" signal.   They do this to determine when a node has been removed from the scene, and their editing capabilities are no longer needed.   For example, the AnimationTrackEditor registers this handler to keep the content "sticky" at the bottom of the screen, regardless of which object is being edited.

The problem with this idiom is that it gets notified of every node removed from the scene.   For example, I clicked on a simple dialog to create a node, and this triggered 600 invocations to the `AnomationPlayerEditor::_node_removed`, this was a dialog that was not related to animations at all, but this callback is invoked for everything.

To make things a little worse, this idiom is not limited to the AnimationEditor, there are 26 instances of this idiom (judging from a quick grep) in the editor.

I believe that we can achieve the same by monitoring `tree_exiting` signal on the node we want to monitor, as it seems to be called in the same condition.

@KoBeWi suggested on the chat room that there is a system in Godot to handle this scenario, but I am not familiar with that system.

### Steps to reproduce

Set a breakpoint in AnimationPlayerEditor::_node_removed, then select an animation in Godot.

Then add an item to the canvas, like "Node2D", tap create, and watch how it takes 600 hits to the callback.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

No minimal project is needed, any project will do.

summary: Revert "EditorResourcePicker: Replace options to load file with button for QuickOpenDialog"

description.text:
- This reverts commit df01daf3b782c2b75537557ad00d564127111ff1 - #97860.

User feedback has shown that this change isn't always a clear improvement, because:
1. The icon eats up horizontal space that's already limited here, cutting the text or previews significantly.
2. The "Load" functionality was completely removed, while it's still useful for many.
3. It breaks user expectations to no longer have load options next to Clear/Save/etc.

Instead of reverting, we could solve (2) easily with a PR like https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/101244, which is an option to consider. (3) is not too bad and users can likely be fine when they understand the new workflow. But (1) seems to be a significant issue to me as seen in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97860#issuecomment-2599105410

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c321a8d7-f556-4eba-ba69-fa9fe0435523)

So given the current timing for 4.4, I suggest we revert for now, and revisit this feature for 4.5. I've also seen positive feedback about the Quick Load button, so we might want to look for ways to bring it back in a way that doesn't clutter the Inspector as much (while keeping the Quick Load and Load PopupMenu entries still). But that requires design work and UX testing that we won't have time to do for 4.4, so it's for 4.5.

- Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/101244
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/101183
- Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11508
- Reopens https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10910

summary: Compute shaders unable to write to UNIFORM_TYPE_IMAGE (uniform image2D) in D3D12

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4beta1 and 4.3stable. 
Tried to do it in 4.2 and the option for d3d12 doesn't exist, I presume that's when directx support was added in the first place. 
Which implies that this maybe has never worked correctly?




### System information

Godot v4.4.beta1.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

When running any compute shader in the D3D12 driver all writing to Image2D seemingly is either not executed, or not retrieval-able from cpu entirely.

This is both in personal projects as well as example projects like the [Compute Shader Heightmap Demo](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/4.2-31d1c0c/misc/compute_shader_heightmap)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download Heightmap Demo: [https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/4.2-31d1c0c/misc/compute_shader_heightmap](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/4.2-31d1c0c/misc/compute_shader_heightmap)
2. Open in any version of Godot that has D3D12 support
3. Set Renderer to Forward+ then restart.
4. Set Driver (Project/General/Rendering/RenderingDevice/Driver.Windows) to d3d12 then restart.
5. Run build in editor and press Create (GPU)

Expected Result (and the result you get on vulkan): 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d3f1979-4d5c-46aa-9653-1165cf984be9)

Actual Result:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8215b4b7-469d-4ed7-961b-7968a21389a7)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

You can download the heightmap demo from the asset store or directly from here: [https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/4.2-31d1c0c/misc/compute_shader_heightmap](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-demo-projects/tree/4.2-31d1c0c/misc/compute_shader_heightmap)

A note, the recorded time elapsed being so similar in both examples might be a result of a significant amount of the time being in syncing/retrieving the image data for viewing in the player on the main rendering thread, and may not be indicative of the compute shader executing correctly. In short I do not know if the compute shader is even being executed.

Also there are no errors resulting from this, or my personal project attempts at using d3d12 with compute shaders, they just don't output any data.

Please note, non-image related compute shaders are functioning, like with my draw indirect PR example project here: [https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/99455](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/99455) which I tested and it ran fine, no errors and grass was placed correctly in the frustum. So this issue seems to be entirely related to Image2D uniforms.

summary: `sample_baked_with_rotation` in Curve3D ignores basis adjustments made with Tilt Handles

description.text:
### Tested versions

Discovered in Godot 4.3.0 Flatpak Release

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Freedesktop SDK 24.08 (Flatpak runtime) - Wayland - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon 780M (RADV GFX1103_R1) - AMD Ryzen 7 7840U w/ Radeon  780M Graphics (16 Threads)

### Issue description

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ef661f0-6050-4772-bce2-9606c9983a0e)
Problem: Result of MultiMeshInstance3D placement (rail ties) along the path using `sample_baked_with_rotation()` for basis. Note the rails follow the tilt handle rotation but the rail ties are following the 'up' indication, not the tilt handle rotation.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7defa243-7751-4bf5-9f3d-70e3570d9e5e)
Expected result: Same MultiMeshInstance3D placement code if basis is computed from `sample_baked_up()` and `direction_to()` a point just ahead on the curve.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Path3D and change the Tilt Handles for some of the nodes.
2. Using scripting, define a MultiMeshInstance3D to instance along the path.
3. If script uses `sample_baked_with_rotation()` to obtain the basis, that basis will not have rotations done by Tilt Handles accounted for, but if the basis is manually computed from `sample_baked_up_vector()` and `direction_to()` a sample_baked just ahead of the current position, the Tilt Handle changes are reflected in the computed basis.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[TrainTracks.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18597402/TrainTracks.zip)
Rails follow the path, as do rail ties if I calculate my own basis using the sample_baked_up_vector(), but if I skip that and instead use sample_baked_with_rotation, the tilt handles aren't accounted for in the basis returned. Switching between these computation modes is toggleable using exported value `use_integrated_basis` in the Path3D node, then selecting another node in the tree to force an update.

summary: RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::get_total_memory_used() is very slow

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.3.stable.mono (2a6663838) - Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS 24.04 - X11 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti (nvidia; 565.57.01) - AMD Ryzen 9 3900X 12-Core Processor (24 Threads)

### Issue description

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6e9c0f3-d81c-40c9-8eb7-a6dd8b6f9ae6)

This function is mainly used for gathering information to then show in [Performance.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ee4acfbfbf15b3528c34a8d4f65d66097e636044/main/performance.cpp#L191), however due to its implementation it's updated every frame in [RenderingServerDefault.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ee4acfbfbf15b3528c34a8d4f65d66097e636044/servers/rendering/rendering_server_default.cpp#L180) even when not needed.

The problem is that [vmaCalculateStatistics](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/thirdparty/vulkan/vk_mem_alloc.h#L1712) is slow (read the doc comment on it for more information).

Ideally, this function would be updated to a more efficient method, or it would only be called when required.

### Steps to reproduce

Launch a large 3d project with a lot of assets (the issue is not as visible in smaller projects)
Attach a profiler
Look at the flame graph

You can use the [TPS Demo](https://godotengine.org/asset-library/asset/678) to see this issue to a certain extent.

summary: LightOccluder2D editor preview is misleading as it darkens the sprite that acts as an occluder

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproductible in: 4.3.stable

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot 4.3.stable - Vulkan (Forward+)

### Issue description

I was trying to cast a light on a Sprite2D that had a LightOccluder2D attached, without actually occluding the sprite itself. In the [documentation](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/2d/2d_lights_and_shadows.html#occluder-draw-order) it is specified that
> LightOccluder2Ds follows the usual 2D drawing order. This is important for 2D lighting, as this is how you control whether the occluder should occlude the sprite itself or not.

This led me to believe that this functionality would work both in the editor and in game. 
When trying this out, **in editor** I got:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0c805097-4b91-46a8-82ef-7ce8bb5723dd)

While testing **the game** I got:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a2a7705-28bb-495b-8ee3-41a97e12117f)

I managed to reach a solution by applying a clockwise cull mode, but that rids me of the flexibility that the occluder ordering relative to the sprite would have given me.

I had initially thought that this is a documentation issue, as I specified in the [godot-docs repo](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs/issues/10578), but @AThousandShips considered that this is likely an engine issue.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a Sprite2D.
2. Click on the Sprite2D, create a LightOccluder2D sibling.
3. Create a PointLight2D which can have a texture or a simple radial black and white GradientTexture2D
4. In the scene tree, change the placement of the occluder to be above, below the sprite or a child that either has or doesn't have 'show behind parent' enabled.
5. No matter which combination was selected, the sprite will always be occluded by the occluder.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18592773/project.zip)

summary: ShapeCast3D incorrectly finds collision points of multiple intersections.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Tested in: v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8], v4.4.dev7.official [46c8f8c5c], v4.4.beta1.official [d33da79d3].
- GodotPhysics and Jolt both have this bug.

### System information

Windows 10 - Vulkan (Forward+) - AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-13600KF

### Issue description

I would expect ShapeCast3D to find correct collision points with multiple colliders at once. Any intersection points (as long as there are no more than max_results) that ShapeCast3D intersects with its shape at its initial creation point should register at the correct position. However, this does not happen with CapsuleShape3D, CylinderShape3D and CubeShape3D. They find the first and last points correctly, but all the others will have erroneous positions.

Both GodotPhysics and Jolt suffer from this.

All experiments were done with multiple CollisionBody3D with BoxShape3D, one above the other, either flush or with a gap (translucent white rectangles in the screenshots) and ShapeCast3D with CapsuleShape3D (yellow capsule), target_position = (0, 0, 0). Different sizes of CapsuleShape3D and colliders were tried.

Features:

1. Points with erroneous positions line up from the first or last correctly found point and go up or down. Attempts to rotate colliders result in these lines rotating together with the colliders. As if the positions of the first and last points somehow affect the others and the rotations of the collider normals are taken into account.

2. ShapeCast3D does not always register the first collider polygon it encounters, but instead registers another polygon from the opposite side, as if the ShapeCast3D direction was in the opposite direction. In the screenshots, pay attention to the collider name (on the side) and the colliding object name opposite the red dot. You can see that some dots are registered on the exit of the collider, not on the entrance. The shapecast direction is top to bottom.

3. Sometimes the points are registered on the edge or on a side polygon with which ShapeCast does not contact at all (4 and 1 screenshots, respectively)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bcee44fc-95a2-43b5-b35b-ed6eeb21f755)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed547c5f-9ad9-48fc-86c6-ac96cbed80a3)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0177557f-51b0-4ba6-befa-42ec7d41a640)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75ab55c5-4630-4ab4-a30c-8a3956b30d57)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f17db9d-4a8b-41ba-9bc6-0b8f7c797657)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9e33b7c-3477-4f04-afb2-3ff68cda8848)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c4155b0-cfcc-432a-9368-54be3f10141e)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e948ee8d-4b7f-4895-bddc-37dd9f0e0a72)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dda9d86f-803d-43b4-b4b5-db6413eeba82)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6c532671-c6b9-4036-8be1-e5992367e8b5)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a 3D scene in a new project.
2. Create a ShapeCast3D:
	- Shape: CapsuleShape3D, radius 0.5 m, height 8.0 m
	- target_position: (0, 0, 0)
3. Create a StaticBody3D with a child CollisionShape3D.
4. CollisionShape3D settings:
	- Shape: BoxShape3D, size = (12.0, 1.0, 12.0) m
5. Duplicate the StaticBody3D several times and move them so that the CapsuleShape3D intersects them in turn.
6. Read all the hit point positions with get_collision_point(i) in a for i: int in range(get_collision_count()) loop and determine if they are in the right places.

About the attached project: made in 4.4 beta 1. I render the collision points using Sprite3D, the camera is set to WASD movement and mouse view, so that it would be easier to see the collision points in 3D space from all sides. The left mouse button can be used to move ShapeCast3D to a new position. CapsuleShape3D is rendered using MeshInstance with a capsule of the same size, so don't forget to change it too if you change ShapeCast3D settings.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[shapecasttest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18591419/shapecasttest.zip)

- 3.x (How the _F_ do you use this thing?!)

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable unknown - EndeavourOS #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 18 Jan 2025 02:26:57 +0000 - Wayland - GLES3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 5500 XT (radeonsi, navi14, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.59, 6.12.10-arch1-1) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 Threads)

### Issue description

When using a terrain set with alternative tiles, the terrain pallet preview tab shows incorrect, unrelated tiles.
This is only present in the pallet previews, not when the tiles are placed in the world.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1bdba08b-d054-461c-9e52-32567b2aca2a)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create 2d scene
2. Add TileMapLayer (named "TileMapLayer_Alt" in MRP)
   2.1. Create new TileSet
   2.2. Assign TileSet to TileMapLayer
   2.3. (optional) Set TileMapLayer CanvasItem>Texture>Filter to Nearest
   2.4. Load tile atlas into TileSet (one is included in the MRP)
3. Create new terrain set for TileSet
   3.1. Set Name to "TEST"
   3.2. (optional) Set Color to your favorite color
4. Paint the terrain set on tiles _excluding_ symmetry variants
   4.1. I.e. In the MRP only paint the _**red**_, skip the _**blue**_
   4.2. Recreate the symmetry variants with alternative tile (included in the MRP)[^1]
   4.3. Paint the terrain set on the alternative tiles
   4.4. Note how in the MRP only red tiles and their alternatives are part of the "TEST" terrain set
5. Go to the TileMapLayer editor 
   5.1. Switch to the Terrains tab
   5.2. Select the "TEST" terrain
   5.3. See ERROR
    - Notice how (in the MRP) even though only red tiles are part of the "TEST" terrain set blue tiles are still part of the preview
 
[^1]: If done correctly, there should be 33 alternative tiles (3, 1, 3, 3, 3, 7, 3, 3, 3, 3, 1)

summary: AMD Radeon "Anti-Lag" feature causes editor stutter

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.3.stable.mono.official [77dcf97d8]
v4.4.stable.mono.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable.mono - Windows 10.0.19044 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700X 8-Core Processor (16 Threads)

### Issue description

I use the default global performance profile on my AMD GPU driver (latest stable release at the time of writing, 24.12.1). This has the feature "Anti-Lag" globaly enabled for all application, and applies it to Godot. (Other default profiles have it also turned on like the Quality Profile). This feature tampers with frame-timing and helps games to run more smooth but it seems to be incompatible with Godot and causes heavy editor stutter on larger scenes. It took me hours to find where the issue is, maybe a warning to the user should be shown, if you think its more of an driver issue than a godot issue?

### Steps to reproduce

* Anti-Lag must be turned on in the driver.
* Any larger scene will start to be choppy and laggy in the editor when moving or zooming. (especially zooming feels like ~10 fps)
* A empty scene or a scene with few objects will not cause this behaviour

Here is a example 144hz recording with a larger scene and turned on&off "anti-lag" where it is visible. (Its only visible on the recoding when you play it on an high refresh rate display!)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9c506112-69b0-4cf3-a0ed-7a32a969479e
